47. Funnel Ads and Marketing Automation Review 2-10-25.mp4
Inner Circle members, welcome in. We're doing a new recurring series. This is one of the first videos in that said series where you can submit your funnels, your marketing automation, and your ads— or any one of those— into the group chat. You can also message it to me. And what I'll do is make a video just like this where we go through it together. And try to give some collective perspective to what's good. What can be improved and again, just hand down some recurring wisdom on a week-over-week basis from this new, this new recurring series that we're gonna add it so we'll call it the 'review call' okay. So the first person that I saw that submitted their information was Nelson. Nelson has a company called VIBES Creative. And...
Let's just try to figure it out together, just like anybody would who gets targeted by it. And let's look at, again, like what's working well in the funnel, what could be improved, and just some overall best practices that might not be being implemented otherwise. So first things first, when we click on this funnel link, we have a video that auto plays. Fun fact: when you have a video that auto plays, it typically does not rank in the search engines. So super pro tip: don't auto play. Videos if you're using Wistia, which it looks like he is using, Vid Vidalytics, Vidalytics can be cool for only really one reason, from my experience, it has this function where, as you're watching the video, the little... What's it called?
The little, like, load bar down here at the bottom? Hides the time of the video and it looks like it's playing like pretty fast, so it gives you the impression it's going to be a shorter video. Then, as the video actually progresses, like the bar slows down dramatically, that's one and honestly, probably the only reason that I actually like Vitalytics, other than that, I always use Wistia. Wistia has the video thumbnails. I'm not sure if Vitalytics has that. If it does, great. You know, another great reason to use it. But I'm a huge fan of Wistia again. Video thumbnails make it look like you know it's a video, but it does not auto-play the video. As a result, that as a result ranks in the search engines.
Also, from what we've experienced at a very high spend level when we did this test, was do we get punished when we have a video that auto-plays? And do we have like other other tech issues? One of the tech issues that occurs as like a recurring theme when you have autoplay videos is on certain mobile devices, more so than anything else. iPhones, um, you'll occasionally get. Somebody that'll click through from an ad and the video is muted. And when they press play on the video, it's like again an autoplay video on mobile browser, depending on what the browser is, it opens up in, it will just mute the video completely. And then, if, as an example, this was on a mobile device, that little uh volume icon was either not there or like in a like a folder menu, like a hamburger menu, where you have to press a button to get the additional menu options to open up, you lose a fuckload of people right away as a result of that.
So to be clear, just to start off, I am not a fan at all of autoplay videos, long story short. All right, so. We have a sub headline at the top, which generally nobody will read. Business owners, founders, and experts. That generally just wastes space, to be clear. I get the point of it. You know, you're trying to qualify your audience and tell the wrong people to just leave, but that's not where people's attention goes right away. People's attention goes is generally on the headline, and in addition to that, it generally goes in this case, especially where the colored part of the headline is good— I actually like this a lot. Where you have this viral podcast system as a highlighted word, because again, you'd be impressed if you actually saw how people behaved.
These web pages there's great tools you can use, like I remember way back in the day, and you can still use this today to be clear. It was lucky orange and then Hotjar came along, giving us the ability to, like, DVR the webpages. The webpages and the like, just sit there and watch recordings of what people would do on our websites and do time and time again. We would see people like hit the page and what felt like scroll down briefly and then leave. At first we were kind of confused, we're like, 'Why is that continuously happening What we then found is we had a survey partner where we would pay a couple thousand bucks to get the webpage in front of the target demographic.
But, like in real life, and what would happen is you would get this feedback that was insane. The feedback would be from real people who were paid to give their feedback on like what they thought of your web site and this was like a little targeted group. It was a very small company at the time, honestly. I've used them in what feels like maybe two or three years. So it's been a while. It's generally not worth it for most companies to do this. It's like a big business already doing like two ish three million dollars a month, give or take a month. So it felt worth it anyway when we had that specific survey company do this and like send us the video recordings of people's responses when they would go to these web pages that we had them looking at.
They would do exactly what I'm describing here: where the person would be like, 'All right, yeah, I read the words viral podcast system, you know, and they were super judgy.' They'd be like, 'Oh, the guy in the bottom left, like, I don't like his hat.' Like, you know, I wouldn't fucking do it. Oh, this looks like too many questions. You know, they would just give you feedback like that. And then they'd be like, 'Yeah,' and then I would just leave. You know, so it's like that's how people really are— to be clear. So anyway, digress. The viral podcast system thing is great— I like that a lot. Okay, the rest of the headline will build and install a viral podcast system in your business, and place the team to run it for you to build authority, become omnipresent, and generate high-quality leads on autopilot.
I feel like this could all be shortened down. to essentially just this we'll build and install a viral podcast system in your business and place the team to run it for you and then the sub headline could be okay so your current sub headline without wasting time doing any editing posting your content yourself or needing a large following that's a pretty good sub headline i don't i don't know i mean look to build authority become omnipresent like i don't know if people ever like when is the last time in real life you've ever heard another business owner that you talk to say some of these words you know like i i literally can't think of one time i've heard somebody say I want to be omnipresent, you know, at a high frequency.
I've heard that, without exaggeration, maybe like a handful of times in my entire life. To build authority, sure, I mean, that's talked about, but you could also just pepper that into this sub-headline here. Anyway. Then you got your video. Again, people might not watch it. Generally, what people are going to do, they'll read that and then they'll kind of scroll down and they'll see if they can get any other information first. Okay, when you've got this right here, I don't like this. I would much rather prefer to see some video thumbnails to make the page a little more interactive. If we go to my Inner Circle page as an example of this. What you'll notice is every single testimonial here has a video thumbnail.
And even more strategic than that, each video thumbnail is— if you just sat there and actually read it, like, what's one thing about getting to the big million dollar month? Okay, 145K. Our business was doing 70K. What do you got today? 145. Cash around 200k, you know, each thing simply put has like meaning in the thumbnail itself, whereas when you look at these thumbnails in particular, it's like they're just people sitting there. And again, it's like it's a static thing. The video thumbnails do a lot to like bring life to people actually caring to watch it. And remember, because we know people aren't going to sit there and watch like, look. This is four minutes. This is three minutes. That's seven minutes. That's four minutes. That's four minutes. That's two minutes.
Almost three minutes. That's four minutes. It's like people aren't going to sit there and watch fucking 30 minutes. The highly analytical, skeptical people might be like, 'Dude, people aren't going to watch that shit.' What they are going to do is they'll briefly read through these quotes that you see below each video. Okay, it is technically a best practice to have the person's name. But if you're going to put their full name like this, what your kind of missing as a pro tip is making their name clickable to their social profile. Because here's the thing: with the names of people, you're more skeptical people who are kind of on the fence and not like just pure risk takers.
They'll look these people up and they'll ask them, like, 'Hey, did you actually have good success with this guy's business So good to know— if you are going to use their full name, you'll notice: I'm not using any full names here. I just pull a quote from the video. Okay. And I make sure that I emphasize like some key thing that happened. And by the way, we had Chad GPT do this. Like, we just took the videos, put them on YouTube. took the YouTube video, ran it through a transcript tool that just pulls the transcript right out of it. Threw that transcript in ChatGPT with a prompt that was like, 'Hey, pull a quote that emphasizes the value of the inner circle.' And these are the quotes that it pulled as a result.
So, anyway, long story short, I would keep that in mind, kind of make this area a little more dynamic. Instead of just the names. I would much rather see this video be like extremely direct at the beginning. That's why I love the mini webinar format so much. You have a clear opportunity here to just call out directly what you can do for people right away at the beginning of the process. Where you say, as an example of this, podcasts work at driving more revenue into your business. As a matter of fact, almost every single big person who makes the most money right now out of all the personal brands has a video podcast. Building them and running them can be a big pain and trying to figure out everything is even more of a pain because you haven't got a lot of time.
But you want the result. You want to get more predictable, qualified people in. To close in your business, you need a podcast. A video podcast— what we do is: We take our viral podcast system, not just a podcast that's going to get filmed and have like a handful of viewers and not actually drive the results that you're looking for, but a podcast that's actually going to get watched. get distributed properly. That's going to be filmed in a very high quality way, increase your presence and authority inside of your niche, and give you a way to generate more predictable revenue from consistent lead flow coming in. I want to help you do this. But more importantly, I want to help staff a qualified team. That's going to run it for you so you don't have to do it yourself.
When you take that type of action in this VSL and that's how you open it up, you can explain all this other stuff after. But you got to remember, like, if you have ever looked at how a VSL is actually consumed, there's a significant drop off at the beginning. It's like a steep cliff. and in the best vs cells, it's more like a slope, but you got to keep in mind— like you got to get off what you can actually do for people immediately. Like you don't want to do this, I think it's a very outdated strategy that people do this where they will use a lot of like framing tactics at the beginning of the vsl and they'll try to, essentially, just to the user.
It's just wasting their time— like they want to know exactly what the fuck you can do for them. And they want you to be somebody that they believe in. You know, they want you to look the part, sound the part, and just come off as convicted and confident, but in a natural way, not like a fake way. And just tell them, 'Look, this is what I can do for you.' And then you do all this framing shit afterwards. I think that would increase the presence of this, you know, quite dramatically. All right. You know, like, instead of adding. That additional friction of taking somebody off the platform it increases your cost per qualified lead a lot when you do so. Okay. Also... This was a very interesting thing that Justesh was sharing last night. He had built this client Rolodex inside of Gamma. And then what he found is, is like when people scrolled towards the... Bottom it eventually just failed and the page would show this like loading error or just like reload towards the top and then he reached out to Gamma support and Gamma was saying that actually not built for mobile at all and that it was only built for like PC and desktop and clearly tablets. I have seen a few of you build very like long Gamma docs like this and not have that issue at all.
So just be mindful of that. Like I know we're all really caught up on how awesome Gamma is, but again, if they have those types of problems, then we're not going to want to use it at all. This is why it's really important to use like a proper video platform, like Wistia or even that Vitalytics tool that we were talking about at the beginning of this. This is likely because, as you just saw, the video's attempting to autoplay. This is a perfect example of the glitchy. shit that can happen on somebody's phone, tablet, or desktop when you try to auto-play a video just be super mindful of that.
We saw that, with I think it was Nelson's Vitalytics video, auto-played just fine. But here we have what looks like a Vimeo video trying to auto-play and it gets blocked. There's a lot of shit going on here on this thank you page. This is after somebody just booked what looks like a podcast strategy session. Nobody's going to pay attention to this because nobody likes to read warning labels. Just keep that in mind. This text. Again, I mean, just because it's near this warning label, I mean, realistically, what's going to happen is people are going to read this headline here at the top and then they're likely just going to move beyond that and kind of start scrolling down the page.
Same type of lesson is what we had already talked about, where you should be posting these videos on Wistia or any other platform that allows video thumbnails and sharing small quotes from the video beneath their names, demonstrating what the person's specific value they got from working with you was, because nobody's probably going to sit here and watch. Any of these you know, let alone a handful of them, at most. So just kind of keep that in mind, you know. It would help a lot in the instance that you chose to do that, especially since you have so many, you know. Yeah, just imagine how much more dynamic this page would be if each one of these was like a video thumbnail with, like, a small quote beneath it.
And it'd also be beneficial, just in terms of impact, to have them stacked on top of each other, like one at a time. That way, you lengthen the page a bit and give more of an opportunity for people to actually read. Because if each one of these had a quote beneath it, it's also a lower probability that people are going to consume it all.
